fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue with high analyst estimates, but currently loss-making and trading at a high forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — significant future growth anticipated.
PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may justify the current valuation.
Total cash $439.70M vs $280.42M debt — manageable leverage.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — the company is not yet profitable.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — inefficient use of capital.
Forward P/E 35.42 — valuation is high, pricing in substantial future growth.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive attention recently, with articles highlighting its substantial 6-month stock performance and discussing its role in AI transceivers. While some analysts question if the rapid ascent has priced in future growth, others remain optimistic about the company's trajectory and large transceiver orders. The narrative suggests a company experiencing a growth spurt, though the sustainability of this momentum is a key question.
